project.creator: Bao
environment: 2. Database: PostgreSQL via TypeORM entities and repositories
3. API entry point: POST /auth/register guarded by CaptchaGuard and ValidationPipe
4. Evidence source: code inspection of auth.controller.ts, auth.service.ts, register.dto.ts, captcha.guard.ts, and email-verification.service.ts
project.notes: Strict feature-based workbook for UC-01 Register Account. The repo does not include an executable auth registration spec, so the cases below are derived from implementation and DTO rules and are currently marked Untested.

changeLog.description: Initial feature/function-based workbook generated from repository auth registration flow and validation rules.

features.name: User Authentication
features.testRequirement: Validate account registration creation, legal consent enforcement, role-based broker skill persistence, duplicate-email protection, CAPTCHA gate behavior, and DTO validation for the UC-01 Register Account flow.

functions.name: Register Account
functions.description: Guest users self-register as CLIENT, BROKER, or FREELANCER through the guarded /auth/register endpoint.
functions.preCondition: The email is not already used, required fields are valid, legal consent is accepted, and CAPTCHA passes when enabled.

testCases.description: Create a CLIENT account successfully with valid data and accepted legal consent.
testCases.procedure: 1. Submit POST /auth/register with a unique email, valid password, full name, phone number, CLIENT role, and acceptTerms = true plus acceptPrivacy = true.\n2. Include a valid recaptchaToken when CAPTCHA is enabled.\n3. Observe the response payload and persisted user state.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The API returns HTTP 201 with a registration success message.
2. A new user is created with role CLIENT.
3. Password is stored as a bcrypt hash.
4. isVerified remains false and email verification is initiated.
5. termsAcceptedAt, privacyAcceptedAt, registrationIp, registrationUserAgent, and currentTrustScore = 2.5 are stored.
testCases.dependencies: Registration endpoint is reachable, email is unique, and CAPTCHA is either disabled or solved successfully.
testCases.note: Derived from AuthController.register and AuthService.register implementation.

testCases.description: Create a BROKER or FREELANCER account and persist selected domains and skills.
testCases.procedure: 1. Submit POST /auth/register with role BROKER or FREELANCER.\n2. Provide valid domainIds and skillIds arrays.\n3. Observe registration completion and follow-up repository writes.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The user account is created successfully.
2. Domain link rows are saved into UserSkillDomainEntity.
3. Skill link rows are saved into UserSkillEntity with priority SECONDARY and verificationStatus SELF_DECLARED.
4. Registration still returns the created auth response.
testCases.dependencies: Valid role is BROKER or FREELANCER and the referenced domainIds and skillIds exist.
testCases.note: Covers role-specific persistence path in AuthService.register.

testCases.description: Reject registration when the submitted email is already in use.
testCases.procedure: 1. Ensure a user already exists with the target email address.\n2. Submit POST /auth/register again with the same email and otherwise valid data.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws ConflictException.\n2. No duplicate user record is created.\n3. No legal-consent timestamps or email-verification work is persisted for the duplicate attempt.
testCases.dependencies: An existing account already uses the submitted email address.
testCases.note: Duplicate-email guard occurs before user creation.

testCases.description: Reject registration when Terms of Service or Privacy Policy consent is missing.
testCases.procedure: 1. Submit POST /auth/register with valid identity fields but set acceptTerms = false or acceptPrivacy = false.\n2. Observe the API response and persistence outcome.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws ConflictException.\n2. No user record is created.\n3. Registration stops before password hashing and verification-email processing complete the flow.
testCases.dependencies: Registration payload is otherwise valid so the consent guard is the failing condition.
testCases.note: Legal-consent enforcement is implemented inside AuthService.register.

testCases.description: Reject registration when CAPTCHA is required but the recaptchaToken is missing or invalid.
testCases.procedure: 1. Enable RECAPTCHA_ENABLED = true.\n2. Submit POST /auth/register without recaptchaToken or with a token that fails verification.\n3. Observe the guard response before controller execution.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. CaptchaGuard blocks the request with BadRequestException.\n2. The register controller action is not executed.\n3. No user record is created.
testCases.dependencies: reCAPTCHA enforcement is enabled in configuration.

testCases.description: Reject malformed registration payloads that fail DTO validation.
testCases.procedure: 1. Submit POST /auth/register with one invalid field such as malformed email, weak password, unsupported role, invalid phone format, or missing required values.\n2. Observe the validation response.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. ValidationPipe rejects the request with HTTP 400.\n2. Clear validation errors are returned for the invalid fields.\n3. No user record is created.
testCases.dependencies: Endpoint uses ValidationPipe with RegisterDto rules.
testCases.note: Covers DTO rules for email, password, fullName, phoneNumber, role, and consent flags.
